DISCOVERY, 302, DEFENDANT LISA KOVACEVICH'S MOTION FOR ORDER COMPELLING PLAINTIFF TO RESPOND TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES; AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS. THE COURT ADOPTED THE TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: THE THREE MOTIONS ARE DENIED. SAID MOTIONS WERE INITIALLY SET FOR HEARING ON MARCH 6, 2015. COURTESY COPIES WERE REQUIRED TO BE DELIVERED TO DEPT. 302 PURSUANT TO LOCAL RULE 2.6B. THE MOVING PARTY FAILED TO DELIVERY SAME. JPT NILS ROSENQUEST CONTINUED THE MOTION HEARINGS TO MARCH 13, 2015 CONDITIONED ON DELIVERY OF MOTION COURTESY COPIES TO DEPT. 302 BY 4:00 PM OM MARCH 6, 2015, AND ADDED THAT FAILURE TO TIMELY DELIVER THE PLEADINGS WOULD RESULT IN DENIAL OF THE MOTIONS. SAID PLEADINGS WERE NOT DELIVERED BY THAT DATE AND IN FACT HAVE NOT EVER BEEN DELIVERED. JUDGE PRO TEM: STEVEN STEIN. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. NOT REPORTED. (302/JPT)
CASE REVIEW REGARDING AWARD OF ARBITRATOR IS OFF CALENDAR - DISMISSAL ON FILE.

calendar.matters:
Motion For An Order Compelling Plaintiff To Respond To Form Interrogatories; And Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Mar-13-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

DEFENDANT LISA KOVACEVICH'S Motion For An Order Compelling Plaintiff To Respond To Form Interrogatories; And Request For Sanctions. The Court adopted the tentative ruling as follows: The three motions are denied. Said motions were initially set for hearing on March 6, 2015. Courtesy copies were required to be delivered to Dept. 302 pursuant to Local Rule 2.6B. The moving party failed to delivery same. JPT Nils Rosenquest continued the motion hearings to March 13, 2015 conditioned on delivery of motion courtesy copies to Dept. 302 by 4:00 pm om March 6, 2015, and added that failure to timely deliver the pleadings would result in denial of the motions. Said pleadings were not delivered by that date and in fact have not ever been delivered.

Judge Pro Tem: Steven Stein. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Not reported. (302/JPT)

Mtn For An Order Compelling Plaintiff To Provide A Response To Defendants Demand For Inspection And Production Of Documents; And Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Mar-06-2015 Continued To Mar-13-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance. The tentative ruling is adopted. DEFENDANT LISA KOVACEVICH's Motion For An Order Compelling Plaintiff To Provide A Response To Defendants Demand For Inspection And Production Of Documents; And Request For Sanctions is continued to March 13, 2015, to give moving party the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Moving party must deliver courtesy copies of the moving papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than March 6, 2015, at 4:00 pm or the motion will be denied. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). The court does not have courtesy copies of any papers. Judge Pro Tem: Nils Rosenquest; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Not reported.
